t_ch: 那太好了！
t_en: That's excellent!

t_ch: 告诉我你主人的秘密！
t_en: Tell me your master's secrets!

t_ch: 放心吧，你们的宝贝就由我带过再创世！
t_en: Don't worry, I'll ensure your treasures survive Era Nova!

t_ch: ——嗷！嗷呜呜！
t_en: Oww! Awoooo!

t_ch: 桀桀，老伙计！惊不惊喜，意不意外？有没有想到在这里也能见到老巴特鲁斯？
t_en: Heh-heh! My old friend! Bet you didn't expect to run into old Bartholos here, of all places!

t_ch: 啊，慢着慢着…看你的眼神，仿佛就是在说——「我还真是活见鬼了？」
t_en: Whoa there, judging by that look, you're probably thinking, "Am I seriously seeing a ghost right now?"

t_ch: 嘿，甭管你是不是真的这么想，但我可以放心大胆地告诉你，根据老巴特鲁斯缜密的推理，此时此刻的我的确是一种鬼魂，是由你的幻想所形成的联觉反应。
t_en: Well, whether or not you are, I can say with full confidence that based on my highly meticulous deduction, I am, at this exact moment, a ghostly figment conjured by your own subconscious.

t_ch: 别误会——第一，咱就是货真价实的巴特鲁斯；第二，货真价实的巴特鲁斯，在「正常时间」中，也确实不会出现在此时此刻的哀丽秘榭。
t_en: Don't get it twisted — first, I am the one and only real Bartholos. Second, the real Bartholos truly wouldn't be in Aedes Elysiae right now, "normally."

t_ch: 桀桀桀，你就姑且认为，能在这里见到老巴特鲁斯是世界的小小奇迹吧！反正类似的奇迹你今后还会见到许多。
t_en: Hehehe... just think of it as a little miracle to see old Bartholos here! You'll start seeing a lot more of us anyway.

t_ch: 好啦，老伙计，咱们言归正传，还是来整点好玩的东西吧！
t_en: Alright, my friend! Back to business, time for a little fun!

t_ch: 咳咳。就像之前每次老巴特鲁斯被抓到之后一样，请您先听我讲个小小的故事。
t_en: Ahem. Like every time old Bartholos got caught, I've got a short story for you.

t_ch: 话说，曾经有那么一次，我在雅努萨波利斯和人玩过这么个游戏……
t_en: See, there was this one time back in Janusopolis, I played a neat little game...

t_ch: 我在人们的面前一字摆出三只箱子；他们呢，只需要交一个金币，就可以去猜我到底在哪一个箱子偷偷藏了宝贝。如果猜对了，宝贝就归他们，打开就能拿走。
t_en: I laid out three boxes in front of a crowd. They only had to pay one coin to guess which one had the treasure. Pick the right one, and the treasure is theirs.

t_ch: 不过，在他们决定好猜哪只箱子之后，我并不会马上公布答案，而是打开一个他们没选的空箱子，然后说，「这只箱子是空的。现在，你要不要改变自己的选择？」
t_en: But after they made their pick, I wouldn't open it just yet. Instead, I'd pop open one of the others — an empty one — and say, "Look! This one's got nothing. Wanna change your pick?"

t_ch: 那肯定改啊！
t_en: Of course, they should change it!

t_ch: 理性的选择！不过…你怎么知道老巴特鲁斯不会一时兴起，偷偷调换了宝物所在的位置呢？
t_en: Smart move! But... how do you know old Bartholos didn't secretly switch the treasure while you were thinking it over?

t_ch: 要坚持最初的直觉。
t_en: Gotta trust your gut and stick with the first choice.

t_ch: 原来如此，您觉得面对老巴特鲁斯的最佳策略就是不为所动！可在这种情况，咱要是真的干干净净，您在概率上可就要吃亏了哦？
t_en: Ah, so your strategy is to stay the course no matter what, huh? Well, if you were playing fair, you'd be hurting your odds, pal.

t_ch: 啊。你问我讲这个故事干嘛？抱着你的疑问，快打开我带回来的这三只宝箱看看吧！看看老巴特鲁斯一时兴起之后究竟会发生什么，桀桀桀桀……
t_en: Ah, wonder why did I tell you all this? Now go open these three treasure chests I brought you and see what happens when old Bartholos gets a little mischievous, hehehe...

t_ch: 桀桀，老伙计！惊不惊喜，意不意外？有没有想到在这里也能见到老巴特鲁斯？
t_en: Heh-heh! My old friend! Bet you didn't expect to run into old Bartholos here, of all places!

t_ch: 啊，慢着慢着…看你的眼神，仿佛就是在说——「我还真是活见鬼了？」
t_en: Whoa there, judging by that look, you're probably thinking, "Am I seriously seeing a ghost right now?"

t_ch: 嘿，甭管你是不是真的这么想，但我可以放心大胆地告诉你，根据老巴特鲁斯缜密的推理，此时此刻的我的确是一种鬼魂，是由你的幻想所形成的联觉反应。
t_en: Well, whether or not you are, I can say with full confidence that based on my highly meticulous deduction, I am, at this exact moment, a ghostly figment conjured by your own subconscious.

t_ch: 别误会——第一，咱就是货真价实的巴特鲁斯；第二，货真价实的巴特鲁斯，在「正常时间」中，也确实不会出现在此时此刻的哀丽秘榭。
t_en: Don't get it twisted — first, I am the one and only real Bartholos. Second, the real Bartholos truly wouldn't be in Aedes Elysiae right now, "normally."

t_ch: 桀桀桀，你就姑且认为，能在这里见到老巴特鲁斯是世界的小小奇迹吧！反正类似的奇迹你今后还会见到许多。
t_en: Hehehe... just think of it as a little miracle to see old Bartholos here! You'll start seeing a lot more of us anyway.

t_ch: 拜拜啦，老伙计！我还在别处给你留了点好玩的东西哦？
t_en: Catch ya later, old friend! I left a few more surprises for you out there, so keep your eyes peeled!

t_ch: 桀桀桀桀！有被吓到吗？
t_en: Hehehehe! Did I spook ya?

t_ch: 唉…咱本想在这里初次登场，好好地吓老伙计一跳，却没成想被老伙计在另一个地方逮了正着。
t_en: *sigh* I was supposed to make a debut here and give you a real scare. But you already bumped into me somewhere else.

t_ch: 不过——咱精心准备的登场位置还是不能浪费！这样这样…作为一种体验上的补偿，老巴特鲁斯就悄悄告诉你一个关于世界的惊天秘密，如何？
t_en: Still, can't let this setup go to waste! Let's see... how about a consolation prize? I'll whisper to you a world-shaking secret. Sound good?

t_ch: 最好真的是惊天秘密。
t_en: It better be actually world-shaking.

t_ch: 黎明机器的真相？那个我知道。
t_en: You mean the truth about the Dawn Device? Already heard that one.

t_ch: 得了吧，我才不信你咧…
t_en: Pfft, no way! Get outta here with that!

t_ch: 桀桀桀桀…咱要讲的秘密，绝对是放之四海而皆准。
t_en: Hehehehe... Nah, what I've got is the real deal. Cross-world certified.

t_ch: 「人生是一个宝箱游戏：一切都可以被隐藏，一切都可以被打开，一切都可以被操纵，一切都可转化成数据。」
t_en: "Life is a game of mystery chests: Anything can be hidden, anything can be revealed, anything can be manipulated... and anything can be turned into data."

t_ch: 你猜，咱们分别在这个比喻的第几层里？
t_en: Can you tell the implication of this metaphor I am referring to right now?

t_ch: 一枚掉在路边的银币，平平无奇。但就在你将它拾起的一刹那，奇怪的手感告诉你，这枚银币其实从未真正落在地面上，而是紧贴地面、保持着一种类似磁悬浮的状态。
t_en: An utterly unremarkable silver coin lies on the roadside. But the moment you pick it up, its strange texture tells you that it never truly touched the ground. Instead, it hovers just above the surface like magnetic levitation.

t_ch: 难道还有一位隐藏的天才？
t_en: Could there be another hidden genius?

t_ch: 别闹了，这只是一个普通的调查事件。从这里发现天才俱乐部秘辛的概率…不能说没有，只能说无限趋近于零。
t_en: Don't be ridiculous. This is just an ordinary investigation. The odds of uncovering any secret from the Genius Society here... Well, let's just say they're infinitely close to zero.

t_ch: 我闻到了宝藏的气息……
t_en: I smell treasure...

t_ch: 一定是扎格列斯干的！
t_en: It must be Zagreus's doing!

t_ch: 仔细观察后，你发现这里有宝箱在地面上压出的痕迹。平白无故消失的宝箱会与谁有关？真是好难猜呢……
t_en: On closer inspection, you notice indentations where a chest once rested. Now it's gone without a trace. Hmm, who might be behind this? That's a tough guess...

t_ch: 看来贼灵也在幻境里…
t_en: Looks like the Spirithief's in an illusion too...

t_ch: 不愧是你，巴特鲁斯。
t_en: As expected, Bartholos.

t_ch: 好耶，去寻宝咯！
t_en: Nice! Let's go on a treasure hunt!

t_ch: 哎哟——投降，投降！再扯下去，我这兄弟俩就要骨肉分离了。呕…这种天旋地转的感觉…太糟糕了！
t_en: Ahh — I surrender, I surrender! If you keep pulling like that, we'll be split apart! Bleh... I feel like the ground is spinning... I feel so sick!

t_ch: 等等…你不是欧洛尼斯的祭司。可是这灰色的一头乱毛…这位{F#美丽的小姐}{M#潇洒的先生}，我们是不是在哪里见过？
t_en: Wait a second... you're not a priest of Oronyx. But that messy gray hair... Say, this {F#lovely miss}{M#dashing sir}, haven't we met before?

t_ch: …你还记得我多少？
t_en: ...How much do you still remember about me?

t_ch: 嘿，你这伙计倒是精明，想来个四两拨千斤，套出老巴特鲁斯的话。
t_en: Heh, you're a clever one, aren't you? Trying to bait old Bartholos into spilling the beans with a flick of the wrist!

t_ch: 见过，在西风的尽头。
t_en: Yeah, we've met. At the end of the west wind.

t_ch: 没有，都是你的错觉。
t_en: Nope, it's all your imagination.

t_ch: 好啦，老巴特鲁斯也知道你不肯说实话。你身上有一种「我们」没有的味道——要是原因和我想的一样，那可就顶有趣啦！
t_en: Hah! I knew you wouldn't spill the truth. But you've carried a scent... one "we" don't share. And if it's what I think it is... oh ho, things are about to get very interesting!

t_ch: 「把才华浪费在正道上实在可惜，您有没有兴趣和我合作合作？」——如果这真是我们第一次碰面，想必我会说出这样的话吧。可惜……
t_en: "It would be such a shame to waste your talents on the right path. Would you be interested in a little collaboration?" — That's what I would've said, if this were truly our first meeting. Pity...

t_ch: 可惜这里只是一场梦，梦外的人不会留在这里，也不可能是我最为期待的犯罪搭档——唉，有缘无份，有缘无份呐！拜拜，一身是谜的朋友！
t_en: Pity this is just a dream, and those outside the dream can never remain here. You were never destined to be the partner in crime I've always longed for... so close, yet so far! Bye-bye, my mysterious friend!

t_ch: 巴特鲁斯一溜烟地跑掉了…看来这位精明的泰坦，已经看破了迷醉的「诡计」。
t_en: Bartholos vanishes in a flash... Seems like this shrewd Titan has seen through the intoxicated "Trickery."

t_ch: 嘿，伙计，你来的正好。你说，人类为什么要发明这么残忍的娱乐活动？
t_en: Hey, buddy, you came at just the right time. Tell me, why do humans invent such cruel entertainment?

t_ch: 残忍？什么残忍…
t_en: Cruel? What are you talking about?

t_ch: 你倒是盯着我多看几眼啊。圆圆的紫葡萄，和圆圆的紫色的我…怎么看都非常相似吧？想想吧，无数个卑微的老巴特鲁斯被你们踩在脚下，绝望地爆开肚皮，流出不可名状的黏液……
t_en: Oh come on, take a good look at me. Big, round, and purple me, looks just like a grape, right? Picture it: countless humble old Bartholoses squashed beneath your feet, bellies bursting in despair, with unspeakable goo spewing forth...

t_ch: 嘶！这得是多么绝望，多么残忍！而你们竟然把它当作游玩项目！
t_en: Tsssk! The horror, the cruelty! And you people even take that as an entertainment!?

t_ch: 嘿，你宝贝的是个天才。
t_en: What a fudgin' genius you are.

t_ch: 桀桀桀，你这不知从哪学来的说话方式还挺有趣！
t_en: Hehehe, what a strange way of talking. Where'd you pick that up?

t_ch: 别自作多情了。
t_en: Don't flatter yourself.

t_ch: 切，一点幽默细胞都没有的家伙。
t_en: Tch. No sense of humor at all.

t_ch: 那是那是，毕竟这个城邦也病得不轻……
t_en: True that. This whole city's pretty sick, after all...

t_ch: 咳咳。不管怎么说，既然你们这么喜欢踩紫色多汁球体的感觉——
t_en: Ahem. Anyway, since everyone seems to love the feeling of stomping on round, juicy purple things—

t_ch: 那老巴特鲁斯长得这么像葡萄，怎么就没人想着踩踩老巴特鲁斯呢？
t_en: Then why's no one ever tried stomping on old Bartholos? I mean, look at me. I'm basically a walking grape here!

t_ch: …你在意的是这个？
t_en: ...That's what you care about?

t_ch: 不愧是你。
t_en: As expected of you.

t_ch: 妈妈，这里有变态！
t_en: Mom, there's a pervert here!

t_ch: 哼，葡萄们被残忍对待也就罢了，难道我巴特鲁斯竟然连被踩扁的资格都没有吗？我都站在这里半天了，从来都没有人正眼看过我！
t_en: Hmph! I could accept grapes getting cruelly crushed, but me, Bartholos, not even qualified to be stomped flat!? I've been standing here for ages, and not a single soul spares me a glance!

t_ch: 现在你总算和我搭话了……桀桀桀，巴特鲁斯决定了，今天不是你被我踩扁，就是我被你踩扁！总之得有人陪我玩才行！
t_en: But now, at long last, you speak to me... Hehehe, that settles it! Either you squash me, or I squash you! One way or another, someone's playing with me today!

t_ch: 药不能停。
t_en: Keep taking your meds.

t_ch: 哎呀哎呀，舒服，畅快！你这家伙，按摩的水平很不错嘛！
t_en: Oh dear. Yeah, right there! You've got talent for massaging!

t_ch: 好了！这下你满意了吧！
t_en: There! Happy now?

t_ch: 搞的像是你赢了似的…
t_en: You act like you won...

t_ch: 你的肚皮好像流不出黏液。
t_en: Seems your belly won't ooze any goo.

t_ch: 什么？难道刚才你真刀真枪的打了？好家伙，看来咱幸亏认输得够快，这才保住了宝贝肚皮，没有让兄弟分家……
t_en: What? You were actually fighting for real just now!? Oh gosh, good thing I surrendered early, or my precious tummy would've gotten split in two...

t_ch: 咳咳。不管怎么说，无人关爱的脚不够长的老巴特鲁斯，这下也算是参与上了娱乐活动。可喜可贺，可喜可贺！
t_en: Ahem! Anyway, the poor old Bartholos, unloved and tragically short-legged, has now participated in a real recreational activity. What a joy! Congrats to me!

t_ch: 唉，遥想当年，巴特鲁斯可是远近闻名的江洋大盗，哪怕是刻法勒或者塞纳托斯，见了咱那也要礼让三分。可现在呢？也许是咱隐身术太好，在这里站个半天，竟然只被你注意到了。
t_en: Back in the day, I was a legendary master thief. Even Kephale and Thanatos would have to show me some respect. But now? Maybe I'm just too good at going invisible. I stood around half the day, and only you noticed me.

t_ch: 虽然人们都说什么「大隐隐于市」…过于无敌还是难免让人空虚寂寞啊。
t_en: People always say "the greatest hides in plain sight"... but being too untouchable makes you feel lonely sometimes, you know?

t_ch: 灰头发的，老巴特鲁斯看你眼熟——以后有事没事多找我玩，记住啊？让咱也享受享受当爷爷奶奶的天伦之乐~
t_en: Hey, gray-hair, you look familiar. Next time you're bored, come hang out with me. Let old Bartholos experience a little about being a grandparent~

t_ch: 咳咳。开玩笑的。如果您不喜欢伦理哏，就忘了刚才这个笑话吧，桀桀。
t_en: Ahem. Just kidding. If family jokes aren't your thing, feel free to erase that one from your memory. Heh heh.

t_ch: 哎呀哎呀，舒服，畅快！你这家伙，按摩的水平很不错嘛！
t_en: Oh dear. Yeah, right there! You've got talent for massaging!
Hee...

t_ch: 咿…耶……
t_en: Yeh... heh...

t_ch: 桀桀？
t_en: Heh-heh?

t_ch: 桀桀。能在一千年以后对上暗号，这就是我们的缘分！
t_en: Heh-heh. To nail the passphrase a thousand years later, what a beautiful twist of fate!

t_ch: 你也…变慢…了吗…
t_en: You've... slowed down... too?

t_ch: 没…事……
t_en: It's... okay...

t_ch: 和…你…玩…玩……
t_en: Just... wanna... play... with... you...

t_ch: 别演我了。
t_en: Drop the act.

t_ch: 桀桀。不愧是你，一眼就能看穿老巴特鲁斯的演技！
t_en: Heh-heh. I knew it, nothing gets past you! Saw right through my act, didn't you?

t_ch: ——那么！接下来要发生什么，你想必也心中有数了吧？
t_en: Which means... you already know what's coming next, don't you?

t_ch: 光明正大的偷袭，桀桀桀桀！
t_en: A perfectly honest ambush! Hehehehe!

t_ch: 哎呀哎呀，相隔了上千年，还是和您交手来得带劲啊！
t_en: Well, well, even after a thousand years, nothing gets my blood pumping like a good brawl with you!

t_ch: 也不知道世界能够延续多久…我是挺希望把你们奋战过的场地都{RUBY_B#塞进肚子}收藏起来{RUBY_E#}，以后卖给…啊不是，以后放在纪念胜利的博物馆里，也算是不枉所有人为此付出的{RUBY_B#时间}一切{RUBY_E#}？
t_en: Who knows how much longer this world's got left in it... But I, for one, would love to collect every battlefield you've fought on and {RUBY_B#swallow them up}preserve them{RUBY_E#}. Later... no no, not to sell, of course, to display them in a victory museum someday! A worthy tribute to all the {RUBY_B#time}sacrifices{RUBY_E#} everyone made, right?

t_ch: 你肚子里还塞得下这个？
t_en: Your stomach's got room for that?

t_ch: 这…不试试怎么知道呢？
t_en: Well, there's only one way to find out.

t_ch: 好一个「时间就是金钱」。
t_en: Taking "time is money" quite literally, huh?

t_ch: 桀桀，那当然了，老巴特鲁斯的宝贝，件件都是开门见山的老！
t_en: Naturally! Every treasure in my collection is as precious as you can imagine!

t_ch: 我不喜欢回忆过去的事。
t_en: I don't like talking about the past.

t_ch: 「过去的事情，就让它留在过去」？桀桀桀桀，那如果「过去」和「未来」竟然是同一件事，你又要怎么取舍呢？
t_en: "Let the past stay in the past"? Hehehe. But what if the past and the future turn out to be the same thing? Then what'll you choose?

t_ch: 不过，咱在这里特意埋伏你，倒也不只是为了贫嘴。你是不是有个水盆不小心忘在过去了？贴心的巴特鲁斯这就把它还你，桀桀桀桀……
t_en: But I didn't lie in wait just to run my mouth. Did you happen to leave behind a basin in the past? Lucky for you, Bartholos is as thoughtful to return it, hehehehe...

t_ch: 我掏…我掏……
t_en: Let me just... dig a bit... dig a bit more...

t_ch: 锵锵！巴特鲁斯向您伸出圆手——古董水盆一件，敬请收好~
t_en: Ta-daa! Behold, Bartholos extends his perfectly round hands and presents you: One antique basin. Please handle with care~

t_ch: 哎呀，救世的大盗，你可要加油啊…我和这盆里的醉鬼，都已经是前世的亡灵，帮不到你什么忙……
t_en: Ah, great thief of Deliverance, best of luck out there... Me and the drunkard in this basin are but spirits of the previous era. We can't help much now...

t_ch: 但你一定能从无趣的命运手中，偷来更有趣的未来，对吧？
t_en: But I know you'll snatch a more thrilling future right out of fate's boring little hands, won't you?

t_ch: 咳咳咳咳，这下咱俩能说上话了。什么？你说为什么不直接搭话？发生了这么多事情，还有人真的在意路边的一串紫葡萄？
t_en: Ahem ahem, now we can finally talk. What? Why didn't I say anything earlier? After everything that's happened, who would care about a bunch of purple grapes on the roadside?

t_ch: 我在意。
t_en: I would.

t_ch: 啊这…您给我整不会了。
t_en: Uh... you really lost me there.

t_ch: 紫葡萄？紫葡萄在哪呢？
t_en: Purple grapes? Where are the purple grapes?

t_ch: 那只是比喻，你就关心吃的了……
t_en: It was just a metaphor, and all you care about is food...

t_ch: 你看，要想引人注意，那只能多多表演啦。
t_en: Look, if you wanna get noticed, you'd have to put on a show.

t_ch: 咳咳…不管怎么说，我现在赢得了你的注意，那自然要充分利用这样的机会。时间的指针即将跨越终点和原初…啊，不用在意这个。
t_en: Ahem... Anyway, now that I have your attention, I should make good use of it. The hands of time are about to cross the end and the beginning... Ah, don't mind that part.

t_ch: 桀桀！走过路过不要错过——这个神秘的房间里有个神秘的宝藏，你唯有借助巴特鲁斯大人的力量才能将它入手！
t_en: Hehehe! Come closer, don't miss your chance. A mysterious treasure lies hidden in this enigmatic room, and only with Lord Bartholos's power can you claim it!

t_ch: 既然我们的友情始于漫长时光之前的一次偶然合作，那么在最终的分别之前…让你体验一下脑控巴特鲁斯是种怎样的感受，应该是一种不错的报偿吧？
t_en: Since our friendship began with a chance collaboration long ago, before we go our separate ways... letting you feel what it's like to mind-control Bartholos sounds like a fair reward, doesn't it?

t_ch: 「脑控巴特鲁斯」？
t_en: "Mind-control Bartholos"?

t_ch: 哦哦，没有别的意思，这是我们意念合一的意思——咳咳，总之不是玩游戏之类的意思，我仍是一个需要尊重次元壁的角色。
t_en: Oh, no, no, don't get me wrong. It just means we were mentally in sync. Ahem, definitely not anything game-related. I'm still a character who respects the fourth wall.

t_ch: 别想借机从我这里偷东西。
t_en: Don't even think about trying to steal from me.

t_ch: 唉呀，瞧您这误会的，这哪能啊…巴特鲁斯对朋友从来都真心相待，遇事更是甘愿为朋友两肋插刀！等等…我好像没有肋条？
t_en: Oh dear, you misunderstood me, big time! How could I ever... Bartholos has always been genuine with friends, ready to take a knife to the ribs for them without hesitation. Wait... do I even have ribs?

t_ch: 好啦好啦，俏皮话咱也不适合说太多…您要是有兴趣，就和咱一起去夺取宝藏吧！
t_en: Alright, alright, enough with the jokes... If you're interested, why not come treasure hunting with me?

t_ch: 桀桀桀…我一直怀疑这东西跟天外世界存在某种联系，既然是您的话，想必一定能够明白我说的是什么意思…其实我自己也不太明白。
t_en: Hehe... I've always suspected this thing has some link to a world beyond the sky. And since it's you, I figured you'd get what I meant... To be honest, I don't really get it myself.

t_ch: 总而言之——走过路过不要错过！这个神秘的房间里有个神秘的宝藏，你唯有借助巴特鲁斯大人的力量才能将它入手！
t_en: Anyway, don't miss your chance! A mysterious treasure lies hidden in this enigmatic room, and only with Lord Bartholos's power can you claim it!

t_ch: 那事不宜迟…
t_en: Then let's not waste any time...

t_ch: 好嘞，得令！
t_en: Ok, command received!

t_ch: 说到底，你为什么不独吞它呢？
t_en: Why didn't you just keep it for yourself in the end?

t_ch: 您真的想知道？长话短说，还是短话长说？
t_en: You really wanna know? Should I give you the long story or cut to the chase?

t_ch: 短的吧。
t_en: Keep it short.

t_ch: 本大爷乐意。
t_en: I feel like it.

t_ch: 长的吧。
t_en: The long one.

t_ch: 桀桀…既然你问出了这个问题，那么巴特鲁斯就用一个小故事来说明吧。
t_en: Hehehe... Since you asked that question, let Bartholos explain it with a little story.

t_ch: 在很久很久以前——啊，也可能就在上个轮回里——有一支来自哈图西里的雇佣兵被困在了奥赫玛城外。
t_en: A long, long time ago... Ah, or maybe it was just the last cycle... a group of mercenaries from Hattusili found themselves stranded outside the city of Okhema.

t_ch: 他们原本被元老院的某些人骗来和刻律德菈阁下作对；但由于那几个元老已然被抓住处刑，这些人突然之间成了一群无家可归的流浪者。
t_en: They had been tricked by a few members of the Council of Elders into going up against Lady Cerydra. But those elders were captured and executed, and the mercenaries suddenly became lost homeless people with nowhere to go.

t_ch: 他们无法继续进军，因为那将是与整个奥赫玛为敌；他们也无法自行回乡，因为轻信了元老的许诺，这些人根本没有携带足够回乡的盘缠。
t_en: They couldn't march ahead, because doing so would make them enemies of all Okhema. They couldn't return home either. They had trusted the elders too easily and brought no money for the journey back.

t_ch: 后来，多亏他们中有个名叫色诺芬的家伙头脑灵活，说「我们各个身强力壮。如果沿途的人们需要体力，我们可以出卖体力；如果沿途的人需要欢乐，我们也可以表演杂技。」
t_en: Later, thanks to a sharp-minded one among them named Xenophon, who said, "We're all strong and fit. If people along the way need labor, we can work. If they want joy, we can perform acrobatics."

t_ch: 据说，世界上的第一个旅行杂技团就这样诞生了；他们从奥赫玛一路表演回了遥远的哈图西里，从此再也没有从事雇佣兵的工作。
t_en: And just like that, the world's first traveling acrobatic troupe was born. They performed their way from Okhema to far-off Hattusili and never went back to mercenary life.

t_ch: 咳咳…故事有点长，不过呢——巴特鲁斯为什么也有和朋友分享战利品的时候？其实就是类似的原因啦。
t_en: Ahem... That story ran a bit long. So why did Bartholos share their treasures with friends? Well, it's for pretty much the same reason.

t_ch: 人们都说世界很大…咱也不能把路走窄了不是？
t_en: People say the world is vast... So why box ourselves in, right?

t_ch: 一个似曾相识的卷轴矗立在你面前。恍惚间，你仿佛置身于希斯拉达恩部落的荒原…不，没有那种事情。这里是翁法罗斯！
t_en: A familiar scroll stands before you. For a split second, you feel like you are standing in the wilds of the Hithradahn tribe... no, that's not right. This is Amphoreus!

t_ch: 但不知为何，你闻到了小猪的气息。不是一只。是三只。
t_en: But somehow, you caught the scent of piggies. Not just one. But three.

t_ch: 和「小猪」玩一下。
t_en: Play with "piggies."

t_ch: 你打算进入「小猪」的房间。小心，别让它们溜走了。
t_en: You're about to enter the "piggies" room. Be careful not to let them escape.

t_ch: 离去。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 你决定不和假冒的小猪一般见识。
t_en: You decide not to stoop to the level of that fake piggy.

t_ch: 桀桀，桀桀。咱就知道你无法抗拒这种诱惑。肥美而不会抵抗的猎物，手到擒来的财宝——正是一切大盗都不会放过的机会！
t_en: Hehehe, hehehe. I knew you couldn't resist such temptation. Plump, helpless prey. Treasure just waiting to be claimed... No thief in their right mind would walk away from that!

t_ch: 蚊子腿再小也是肉。
t_en: Even crumbs are worth grabbing.

t_ch: 没错没错！这正是江洋大盗最重要的精神。
t_en: That's right! This is the true spirit of being a master thief!

t_ch: 得了吧，我早就玩腻了。
t_en: Oh please. I've been bored of this for ages.

t_ch: 话虽如此，但我们还是放不下那些无人发掘的财宝，对吧？
t_en: Still, we can't help chasing after unclaimed treasures, can we?

t_ch: 巴特鲁斯听说，有位城邦哲学家曾得出结论——世界的本质就是「吃」！植物贪婪地吮吸阳光，动物更是不放过一切自己能消化的东西。至于人或者泰坦——
t_en: I heard about this city-state philosopher who figured out that the nature of the world is basically just "eating"! Plants soak up sunlight like they're starving, animals gobble up anything they can digest. As for humans or Titans...

t_ch: 「于万物主宰有大信心者，可以吃一切存在之事物！」桀桀…暴食的形骸是可鄙的，暴食的灵魂却是高贵的！
t_en: "Those who truly believe in the ruler of all things may feast upon whatever exists!" Hehehe... The physical act of gluttony might seem disgusting, but the spirit behind it is absolutely noble!

t_ch: 记得拿走你遇到的每一份财宝，让我们无愧于大盗之名！
t_en: Grab every treasure you spot. We've got our reputation as infamous thieves to maintain!

t_ch: 天凉了，该把所有的记忆付之一炬了。
t_en: It's getting colder now. Time to burn all memories to ash.

t_ch: 从名字到台词，这只桶充满了盗用创意的气息。在一个盗贼亦可成为泰坦的世界，模因被偷盗似乎也是迟早的事情。
t_en: This trashcan's entire identity, from name to dialogue, screams plagiarism. In a world where thieves can become Titans, stealing memetic entities was just a matter of time.

t_ch: 现在的宝箱，真是一个比一个无趣了。三秒钟就能开锁，这怎么够本大爷拯救财宝的仪式感呢？
t_en: Chests these days are so boring. Three seconds to unlock? How's that supposed to satisfy my sense of ceremony of rescuing treasures?

t_ch: 结束宝箱暴政！
t_en: Down with chest tyranny!

t_ch: 没错没错！包括你的背包！
t_en: That's right! Your backpack included!

t_ch: 这模仿有够拙劣。
t_en: Seems like a cheap knockoff.

t_ch: 桀桀，您说得对，咱还就是这个味儿！
t_en: Hehehe, you're right! That's just how I roll!

t_ch: 你被桶踢了一脚。
t_en: You got kicked by the trashcan.

t_ch: 桀桀，桀桀。你的记忆当中还真是能偷出些相当有趣的东西。
t_en: Hehe. Your memories hold some absolutely fascinating things worth stealing.

t_ch: 你偷窥我的记忆？
t_en: You're flipping through my memories?

t_ch: 这咱倒是想…但可惜做不到。
t_en: I certainly wish I could... but unfortunately, I can't.

t_ch: 师父，快教我怎么偷记忆！
t_en: Master, quick! Teach me how to steal memories!

t_ch: 嗐，这还不简单？
t_en: Ha, that's easy!

t_ch: 嗐，这还真不简单！咱也做不到。
t_en: Ha, it's actually quite complicated! Even I can't do it.

t_ch: 说到底，咱只是一个普通的盗贼，可以偷你「一直拥有」的记忆要素，但对你「一瞬拥有」的真正记忆就无可奈何了。
t_en: Let's be honest. I'm just a regular thief who can steal elements of the memory fragments you "always carry," but those authentic memories you "hold for just an instant" are completely beyond my reach.

t_ch: 啊——咱不是说你的记忆有什么问题。每个人的记忆都是这样…过去并非一帧不差地留在了你们的心里。
t_en: But I'm not saying there's anything wrong with how you remember things. Everyone's memory works the same way... The past doesn't just sit there in your mind like some perfect recording.

t_ch: 你们所保留的，是类似我们的世界这种，由形形色色的模因交织而成的虚拟现实。
t_en: What you keep is really just a virtual reality crafted from various memetic entities, much like our world.

t_ch: 无论拍摄留影，还是动笔记录，人类并非泰坦，不可能保有绝对真实的过去。消逝的细节就像沙粒一样从指缝之间失去……
t_en: You see, humans aren't Titans. No amount of photography or record-keeping can capture the absolute truth of what happened. All those tiny details just slip through your fingers like sand...

t_ch: 不过可别担心！只有把浮土全部吹去，真金才会被我们握在手心。
t_en: But don't you worry! We only find the gems after we've blown away all the worthless dust.

t_ch: 嗯？所以到底什么是真金？那当然是巴特鲁斯也没法从你心里偷走的「真正的记忆」啦！
t_en: What gems, you ask? Those "authentic memories" that even I, Bartholos, can't possibly steal from your heart!

t_ch: 这种记忆不存在于过去，不存在于记录，只存在于你回忆起它的一瞬，存在于你心头涌起的特殊感情——桀桀，这种东西即便是泰坦那也偷不走啦！
t_en: These precious memories don't exist in the past or in records. They only exist in the exact moment you recall them, in those special feelings that flood your heart... Hehehe, such things are beyond anyone's reach, even Titans!

t_ch: 锵锵，是紫色的小猪，有毒的小猪！
t_en: Ta-da! Meet the purple pig, the poisonous little piggy!

t_ch: 没想到吧，桀桀桀桀——
t_en: Surprised you there, didn't I? Hehehehe—

t_ch: 桀桀…古有王下一桶，今有盗下一圣！
t_en: Hehehe... History had its Lordly Trashcan, and now it has the Thievishly Saintcan!

t_ch: 桀桀，看这边，看这边。我听说，从这里出去就能离开翁法罗斯…这事儿是真是假？
t_en: Hehehe, look this way, over here. I heard you can get out of Amphoreus through here... is that true?

t_ch: 没有的事。
t_en: Not true.

t_ch: 嗐，你可骗不过「诡计」的祖师爷！也就是说，或许你们能从这里出去，但巴特鲁斯就绝无可能？
t_en: Hah, you can't fool the legend of "Trickery"! So you're saying that while you folks might make it out, Bartholos has absolutely no chance?

t_ch: 确实如此。
t_en: Indeed.

t_ch: 哎呀，那真好啊。可惜，巴特鲁斯不是天外来客，你们的黑客后门，对我来说毫无用处。
t_en: Oh my, that sounds nice. Too bad ol' Bartholos isn't from beyond the sky, your hacker backdoor is useless to me.

t_ch: 不告诉你。
t_en: Not telling you.

t_ch: 切，小气。不过想也知道，巴特鲁斯不是天外来客，你们的黑客后门，对我来说毫无用处。
t_en: Tch, stingy. But obviously, ol' Bartholos isn't from beyond the sky, so your hacker backdoor is useless to me.

t_ch: 你别说…咱有时候就想，对于更外面的世界来说，翁法罗斯是不是就像一个大大的宝箱？要是这样，和咱们作对的铁墓，大概就相当于一只讨厌的宝箱怪吧！
t_en: Y'know what... Sometimes I wonder if Amphoreus is like one big treasure chest to the outside world? If that's the case, then Irontomb, our enemy, would be nothing but an annoying mimic!

t_ch: 不过这么说来，能被一只小小的宝箱怪威胁…外面的宇宙还真是病入膏肓了呀。你要走的道路还真是艰难呢，来自天外的朋友！
t_en: Come to think of it, being threatened by a tiny mimic... The universe out there must really be in dire straits. You've got your work cut out for you, my friend from beyond the sky!

t_ch: 你好啊，帅气的银河飞行员！您的老朋友巴特鲁斯也在参与飞行员培训课程——
t_en: Well, hello there, dashing cosmic pilot! Your old friend Bartholos here is also taking the pilot training course...

t_ch: ——才怪。被困在这些讨厌的柱子中间，我都快变成安提基色拉人的动力核心了！
t_en: ...Just kidding! Being stuck between these awful pillars, I feel like I'm about to become the Antikytherans' energy core myself!

t_ch: 所以，怎么回事？
t_en: So, what's going on?

t_ch: 你知道这些柱子是什么吗？桀桀，它们是支撑翁法罗斯的服务器——我猜是这样。
t_en: You know what these pillars are? Heheh, they're the servers that keep Amphoreus running, or so I'd guess.

t_ch: 那么，祝你玩得开心。
t_en: Well, have fun, then.

t_ch: 喂喂，我现在可是卡在了翁法罗斯的服务器上唉！关心一下你的老伙计啦。
t_en: C'mon, I'm literally stuck inside the Amphoreus servers here! Spare a thought for your old pal here, will you?

t_ch: …诶，对了对了——咱们朋友一场，你就行行好，帮我把剩余的「培训课程」做完如何？
t_en: Oh, I know! Since we're pals and all, how about you do me a solid and finish up the rest of this "training course"?

t_ch: 我受不了这些该死的机关，它们锁住的大宝箱就归你了！只要早点把我放出来就好……
t_en: I can't deal with these blasted mechanisms anymore. The big chest they're locking up? It's all yours! Just get me out of here as fast as you can...

t_ch: 桀桀桀，亲爱的伙计，看在{RUBY_B#列位星神}天外诸神{RUBY_E#}的份儿上，巴特鲁斯向您致以崇高的问候！
t_en: Hehehe, my dear friend, by the {RUBY_B#Aeons}gods beyond the sky{RUBY_E#}, I, Bartholos, send you my most noble greetings!

t_ch: 别闹了，快把短矛还我
t_en: Stop playing games and give me back my spear.

t_ch: 短矛我会还你，不过…你瞧，月色如此美丽，老巴特鲁斯也忍不住想与老朋友在月下攀谈——
t_en: You'll get your spear, but first... just look at that beautiful moon. It makes me want to have a nice chat with an old friend.

t_ch: 虽然由咱来提这个问题兴许有点奇怪，不过，作为漫长旅途中的小小一站……
t_en: I know it's a bit weird for me to be asking, but since this is just a little stop on your long journey...

t_ch: 翁法罗斯，是否让您留下了遗憾？
t_en: Do you have any regrets about your time in Amphoreus?

t_ch: 是
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 桀桀…甚好、甚好。扎格列斯老祖说过，遗憾是爱的证明。
t_en: Heheh... Excellent, excellent. As ancestor Zagreus used to say, "Regret is proof that you ever loved."

t_ch: 诡计的泰坦，偷走一切又归还一切，目的也不过是用失去来唤醒珍惜。
t_en: The Trickery Titan, who takes all only to give it all back, only to teach us to appreciate what we have through the pain of loss.

t_ch: 否
t_en: No.

t_ch: 桀桀…甚好、甚好。如此我们便能轻装前行，各赴前程。
t_en: Heheh... Excellent, excellent. Then we can move on with light hearts, each to our own destiny.

t_ch: 巴特鲁斯会买下奥赫玛的一整条街，以后想偷哪家就偷哪家；您呢，以后要是想回来坐坐，举会儿黎明机器玩玩也不在话下……
t_en: I'll buy a whole street in Okhema to rob any place whenever I want. And you, if you ever want to come back and visit, you can play with the Dawn Device all you want...

t_ch: 咳咳…天机不可泄露，老巴特鲁斯一不小心好像又说得太多。好啦，咱说话算话——
t_en: Ahem... Some secrets are better left unknown. Looks like ol' Bartholos got carried away again. Anyway, as promised...

t_ch: 短矛还你，有空记得回来玩啊！
t_en: Here's your spear back. Do come back and hang out again sometime!

t_ch: 月光、池水、翻飞的金币…扎格列斯老祖说过，「无所事事的夜晚，连思考都是罪过」。
t_en: Moonlight, the pool, coins flipping in the air... Our ancestor Zagreus once said: "The slightest thought is a sin on an idle night."

t_ch: 真让人恍如隔世
t_en: It really feels like a lifetime ago.

t_ch: 桀桀，还记得扎格列斯老祖的另一句名言吗？「行窃，只是取回本该属于自己的东西」。
t_en: Heheh, remember another one of ancestor Zagreus's famous sayings? "Theft is but retrieving what belonged to you in the first place."

t_ch: 如果不算那些留着金血的英雄豪杰，毫无疑问——咱巴特鲁斯，就是在这{RUBY_B#翁法罗斯}方寸果壳{RUBY_E#}里陪伴了您最久的朋友。
t_en: If you don't count those heroes with golden blood, then without a doubt: I, Bartholos, have been your longest companion in this little {RUBY_B#Amphoreus}nutshell{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 哦，对了对了…说到这个，就让老巴特鲁斯再告诉您一个小小的秘密……
t_en: Oh, yes, that reminds me... allow ol' Bartholos here to let you in on one more tiny little secret...

t_ch: 这里是巴特鲁斯为自己精心挑选的归隐之地。换句话说…如果您哪天觉得自己手头不够宽裕…回这里翻翻，巴特鲁斯的财宝也是您的财宝！
t_en: This is the secluded retreat that I hand-picked for my retirement. In other words... should you ever find your coin purse a bit light, feel free to come back and rummage around. What's mine is yours!

t_ch: 回见
t_en: See you later.

t_ch: 回见！到时候，请允许老巴特鲁斯再偷走您的一点儿时间~
t_en: See ya! When we meet again, allow me to steal just another moment of your time~

t_ch: 你要不要我的翻飞之币
t_en: Want my Coin of Whimsy?

t_ch: 桀桀，那当然要啦！巴特鲁斯特别为您准备了一些「好东西」——瞧一瞧，看一看，要是钱不够了还可以找扎格列斯去换…哦，我是说扎格列斯之手，还有类似的那些东西。
t_en: Hehehe, of course! Bartholos has prepared some "good stuff" specially for you! Take a look, have a peek, and if you're short on money, you can always trade with Zagreus... oh, I mean the Hand of Zagreus, and things like that.

t_ch: 你面前的大家伙纹丝不动。仔细一看，大家伙的旁边压着一张字条……
t_en: The huge entity in front of you won't budge. Upon closer inspection, you see a note pinned next to it...

t_ch: 「如果您又遇到了困难，请大喊一声『帮帮我，巴特鲁斯！』」
t_en: "If you run into trouble again, just give a shout: 'Help me, Bartholos!'"

t_ch: 「——给你的老伙计留一个踩着七彩祥云来救你的机会吧，桀桀。」
t_en: "Do give your old pal a chance to swoop in and save the day like a hero does, heheh."

t_ch: 帮帮我，巴特鲁斯！
t_en: Help me, Bartholos!

t_ch: 哦，当然，巴特鲁斯随时随地响应您的召唤！
t_en: Oh, absolutely! Bartholos, at your service, anytime, anywhere!

t_ch: 你决定不给巴特鲁斯这个机会。
t_en: You decide not to give Bartholos this chance.

t_ch: 嗝~白煮蛋要剥壳才能吃，箱子里的宝物也一样，让巴特鲁斯消化一下才能乖乖听话。
t_en: Burp~ You've gotta peel a hard-boiled egg before you eat it, and it's the same deal with treasure in a chest. O' Bartholos gotta digest it a little before it'll behave!

t_ch: 等等——这么说来，老巴特鲁斯莫非是海胆的亲戚？不对不对，海胆喜欢戴帽子，但老巴特鲁斯并不喜欢。事了拂衣去，深藏功与名……帽子还是太过拉风了点。
t_en: Wait a second! Does that mean I'm related to sea urchins? No, no, that's not it. Sea urchins love wearing hats, but I don't. I do my thing and disappear right after, keeping my contributions and reputation unspoken... Hats are just too flashy for my style.

t_ch: 咳咳…不管怎么说……
t_en: Ahem... Anyway...

t_ch: 「无聊的真相个个相似，精彩的诡计个个不同」——送你一句扎格列斯老祖的格言，咱们有缘再会~
t_en: "Boring truths are all the same, while every brilliant trick is unique in its own way." Here's a little proverb from ancestor Zagreus for you. Until we meet again~

t_ch: 紫色的大胃袋包裹了你……
t_en: The purple stomach pouch engulfs you...

t_ch: 关于「黄金替罪羊」……
t_en: About the Golden Scapegoat...
...Shh!

t_ch: 这跟平常的口嗨可不一样，咱们换个人少的地方再聊。
t_en: This is different from our usual banter. Let's go somewhere quieter to talk about this.

t_ch: 不过——原来我一直等待的合作伙伴就是你啊！不错不错、这可真是太好了！
t_en: But... I guess you're the partner I've been waiting for. This is great news! Excellent news!

t_ch: 恩维尔带你来到了一个相对僻静的角落……
t_en: Avilius takes you to a relatively quieter corner.

t_ch: 嗯，这里就方便说话多了。
t_en: We can talk here.

t_ch: 呃，至于这么神秘吗？
t_en: Um, is this necessary?

t_ch: 倒也不是？只是重要的事情当然要追求点仪式感嘛！
t_en: Not really, but it's important enough to warrant a sense of seriousness!

t_ch: 怎么感觉像打劫似的…
t_en: I feel like I'm being robbed...

t_ch: 诶，别这么开玩笑啊…就我这小身板，怕不是叠起来十个也不够你一棒子打？
t_en: No way! You can take on ten of me easily!

t_ch: 总之，既然你向我打听起了「黄金替罪羊」——这是我自己秘密给它们取的名字——你也已经通过了最基础的4关，是这样没错吧？
t_en: Anyway, since you asked me about the Golden Scapegoat — a name that I came up with secretly — that means you've cleared the four basic stages as well, right?
As well?

t_ch: 哦，你……
t_en: Oh, you mean...

t_ch: 嗯，我自己也试过挑战它，侥幸通过了不那么凶险的几关。也正因为如此，我才弄到了这个——在开始挑战或者点亮世界的时候，我们能听到的那些古泰坦语的翻译。
t_en: Yes, I challenged them too, and was fortunate enough to pass the ones that weren't so dangerous. That's how I got this — the translations of the ancient Titan language that we can hear when the challenge starts or when the world is illuminated.

t_ch: 怎么样，是不是感觉它背后似乎有一些…神神秘秘的东西？比如，屠龙者终将成为恶龙，传火者终将成为灰烬……
t_en: Well? Feels like there's something... enigmatic behind it, right? For example... The dragonslayer inevitably becomes the evil dragon, and the torchbearer inevitably becomes ashes...

t_ch: 那谁知道啊。
t_en: I have no idea.

t_ch: 确实，即使是负责翻译这些文本的那位学者前辈，她自己也无法笃定这一点。
t_en: That's true. Even the scholar who translated this couldn't be sure about it.

t_ch: 不至于吧…就这种朦胧诗？
t_en: Really? She couldn't be sure about these misty poems?
Um...

t_ch: 好吧，如果你是在意这里面的语言风格…那我必须说明，这都是翻译的结果。原文是我还不太能搞懂的古泰坦语，是不是真的也是这种风格，就只有那位帮助我的学者前辈自己知道了。
t_en: Well, if it's the style that concerns you... just know that all of this is translated from the ancient Titan language, which I don't fully understand yet. Only the scholar knows if the original was also written in the same style.

t_ch: 是有点…不知该怎么形容。
t_en: It's a little... How should I put it?

t_ch: 对吧，有种灰暗而深沉的味道…但又充满了诗意的能量！
t_en: Right. It gives off an energy that's a little gloomy and deep... but extremely poetic!

t_ch: 无论如何，这些都是从「黄金替罪羊」赐予我们的战利品中提取、翻译出来的宝贵信息——我相信它们一定有着非比寻常的特殊意义。
t_en: Anyway, they're all precious information extracted and translated from the treasures bestowed upon us by the Golden Scapegoat, and I firmly believe that they are of unusual significance.

t_ch: 比如第一段里提到，「火与光即是那罪的化身」——这段话会不会是指那些伟大的黄金裔？
t_en: Just look at the first stanza. "Fire and light are the incarnation of that sin." Is this referring to the great Chrysos Heirs?

t_ch: 也许他们的救世计划，会在无形之中对世界造成某种讳莫如深、但又无可奈何的损害？他们是否会因此深深心痛，却又无法对我们这些世俗大众作任何辩解？
t_en: Maybe their plan to save the world will somehow cause irreversible damage to the world, but we must bear it no matter what? Are they hurting because of it, but are unable to explain anything to us mortals?

t_ch: 又比如这第二段，「我毕竟对屠刀一无所知」——信息的来源似乎相信有种来自神秘领域的不可见的力量在威胁着整个世界…但会是什么呢？
t_en: Also, in the second stanza, "After all, I know nothing of the butchering blade." The source of the information seems to believe that an unseen power from a mysterious domain is threatening the world... But what could it be?

t_ch: 还有还有，第四段最后的那句——「神明想看见世界：因此便有了{RUBY_B#光明}黑暗{RUBY_E#}」…在这里，前辈无法准确把握的泰坦语词意出现了两种截然相反的意思…那究竟代表什么呢？
t_en: Oh, and the last line of the fourth stanza... "The god wishes to see the world, and so {RUBY_B#light}darkness{RUBY_E#} came into being." The scholar was unable to precisely interpret the meaning of the Titan word here, and it contains two opposing interpretations. What does that mean?

t_ch: …难道说，在古泰坦语的某些特殊表达方式里，光明和黑暗——哦，还有时间——都可以用同一个词来指称？
t_en: ...Could it be that, in the ancient Titan language, light and darkness — Oh, and also time — are represented by the same word?

t_ch: 对此，那位前辈和我都无法做出任何经得起推敲的解释。或许只有从不同的挑战中记录下更多不同的这类低语，我们才可能解答自己的疑问…当然也可能带来更多的疑问就是了！
t_en: The scholar and I couldn't come up with any explanations that withstood scrutiny. Maybe the only way for us to answer it is to record more such whispers from the different challenges... But that might also bring about more questions!

t_ch: 果然是阴谋论啊。
t_en: What a conspiracy.

t_ch: 诶，这怎么是阴谋论呢！你明明也在攻略「黄金替罪羊」，我不信你会觉得那里面没藏着任何惊天秘密。
t_en: It is not a conspiracy! You're trying to conquer the Golden Scapegoat too, right? You can't possibly think that there are no surprising secrets within.

t_ch: 我很忙，你捡重点讲吧。
t_en: I'm busy. Please just get to the point.

t_ch: 哦哦，简单来说，就是我认为「黄金替罪羊」里面藏着了不得的秘密——但包括我自己在内的任何人都还不知道那是什么！
t_en: Basically, I think there's an incredible secret hidden in the Golden Scapegoat, but no one knows what it is, including me!

t_ch: 你慢点…说这么多我记不住。
t_en: Slow down... I can't keep up.

t_ch: 啊…呃，是我一下说得太多了吗？
t_en: Um, did I speak too much?

t_ch: …这样，我稍后把你拉进我们在传信石版上的秘密讨论组；只要你攻略到更多的「黄金替罪羊」，那位学者前辈就能阶段性地把自己新的翻译成果发在讨论组里——
t_en: ...Well, why don't I add you to our secret discussion group on the teleslate? Once you overcome more of the Golden Scapegoats, the scholar will be able to send her newer translations to the group.

t_ch: 嗯…比起经常在现实中碰头，还是这样更安全一些。
t_en: It's much safer compared to meeting up in person frequently.

t_ch: 哦，单线联系的秘密调查！
t_en: Ah, a secret investigation with a single point of contact!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，你果然也是懂的——这种有点禁忌氛围的研究才最带感嘛！
t_en: Yes, you get it! It's more exciting if the research feels a little forbidden, right?

t_ch: …为什么不能光明正大一点？
t_en: ...Why can't we be more open about it?

t_ch: 我倒是也希望…不过那位学者前辈专门叮嘱过我，此事或许与世界背后的本质规律有关，在真的发现什么之前最好不要张扬……
t_en: I would like to, but the scholar specifically warned me that the research might be linked to the underlying rules of the world, and that we shouldn't talk about it openly before we make concrete discoveries...

t_ch: 你说什么都行，因为我没兴趣。
t_en: Whatever, I'm not interested.

t_ch: 诶，别这么说嘛！不管你嘴上怎么说，你的脚都已经走到这一步了不是？
t_en: Don't say that! You're here already, aren't you?

t_ch: 好了…在这里嘀嘀咕咕太久难免引人生疑——我们下次还是在传信石版上接头吧！溜了溜了，在刻法勒广场上见面的时候，可别说我们聊过这个神秘话题啊！
t_en: Anyway, people might start suspecting us if we stay here any longer. Let's talk on teleslate next time! Don't mention this conversation when I see you in Kephale Plaza!

t_ch: 恩维尔像一溜烟似的跑得无影无踪。
t_en: Avilius quickly disappears.

t_ch: 关于「黄金替罪羊」……
t_en: About the Golden Scapegoat...

t_ch: 哈哈，你是因为前辈在群里发的消息来的吧！
t_en: Ha, you're here because of the messages from the teleslate, right?

t_ch: 说实话，关于咱们到底能不能从这些挑战里发现某些古代不为人知的秘密…我和前辈的想法并不一样。
t_en: To be honest, I don't agree with the scholar on whether we can discover unknown secrets of the past from these challenges.

t_ch: ——怎么样，还是像上次那样找个僻静之处，我跟你好好说道说道？
t_en: Well? What do you say? Should we go somewhere quiet like last time so I can tell you what I think?

t_ch: 行吧……
t_en: Fine...

t_ch: 得了，把最后一段给我就行。
t_en: Just give me the last stanza.

t_ch: 呃。
t_en: Um.

t_ch: …好吧，给你。
t_en: ...Alright, you can have it.

t_ch: 这就是我们已知的最后一段。希望它已经能满足你的好奇心。
t_en: This is the last known stanza, I hope it quenches your curiosity.

t_ch: 未来…我会找到更强有力的证据来说服所有人。
t_en: Going forward... I'll find more concrete evidence to convince everyone.

t_ch: 恩维尔带你来到了一个相对僻静的角落……
t_en: Avilius takes you to a relatively quieter corner.

t_ch: 哎呀，你愿意听我细细讲来，这实在是太感谢了。
t_en: Thank you so much for being willing to listen to me.

t_ch: 我有一个大胆的假设——一旦它成立，或许世界上所有的未解之谜就全都说的通了！
t_en: I have a bold hypothesis that, if proven true, can explain just about every unsolved mystery in this world!

t_ch: 其实……
t_en: Well...

t_ch: 我们每个人都是「忘记了自己是泰坦」的泰坦！是的，在过去，人类和泰坦的关系不仅仅远比现在更亲密，甚至根本就是同一存在！
t_en: We are all Titans who have forgotten our identity as a Titan! That's right. Humanity and the Titans had a much closer relationship in the past, to the point where we were one and the same!

t_ch: 啊？这么跳跃的？
t_en: That's... a huge leap.

t_ch: 嗐，你听我解释嘛。
t_en: Let me explain.

t_ch: 逻辑：人是由自我定义的物种。
t_en: Logic: Humans are a species defined by itself.

t_ch: 嗯……差不多是这个意思？不过为了避免误会，你还是听我解释一遍吧。
t_en: Umm... Kind of? But let me explain so you don't misunderstand me.

t_ch: 您搁这儿玩绕口令呢。
t_en: I'm not interested in mental gymnastics.

t_ch: 嗐，你听我解释嘛。
t_en: Let me explain.

t_ch: 为什么老师翻译出来的文本总是让人似懂非懂？为什么这种据说属于泰坦的语言如此模棱两可，以至于「光明」和「纷争」、「时间」和「罪恶」竟然在用同一个词汇表达？
t_en: Why are the translations from the scholar so fuzzy? Why is this language, supposedly having been used by the Titans, so vague that "light" and "strife," and "time" and "evil," are expressed by the same word?

t_ch: 答案就在这里。因为我们忘记了人类也是泰坦，忘记了这种语言不是通过耳朵和眼睛，而是通过心灵去理解的。
t_en: The answer lies in my hypothesis. It's because we have forgotten that humans are Titans too. We forgot that the language must be understood by the soul, instead of ears and eyes.

t_ch: ——也就是说，我们必须换一种方式，用心灵去阅读「黄金替罪羊」的遗存，这样才能真正理解它当中蕴含着的深意。具体来说，就是……
t_en: ...In other words, we must read the legacy of the Golden Scapegoat with our soul to truly understand its significance. And that is...

t_ch: 恩维尔不知从哪里搬来了一张躺椅。
t_en: Avilius brings a lounge chair out of nowhere.

t_ch: 我决定用一顶由细铜丝特制的帽子，把自己的头皮和黄金替罪羊零距离连通…然后在这里睡上一觉，试试能不能用梦境和世界背后的真实沟通！
t_en: I've decided to connect my scalp directly to the Golden Scapegoat with a hat made from thin copper strands. I will then take a nap here to see if I can communicate with the truth behind this world in my dream!

t_ch: 这集我是不是在哪里看过。
t_en: Have I seen this somewhere before?

t_ch: 好一个…格物致知。
t_en: You're really getting to the bottom of this, aren't you?

t_ch: 诶，凡事总要试试才知道有没有价值吧？如果演化神秘学的理论没错，那么人类或许至今都还保留着泰坦的全部感官，只是退化之后，自己完全意识不到罢了。
t_en: How will I know if I don't try? If Evoccultism is correct, modern humans still possess all the Titan senses, but we are unaware of them because these senses have atrophied.

t_ch: 说到「自己无法控制、甚至意识不到」的感官体验，那最容易想到的就是「做梦」了吧？
t_en: And naturally, the sensory experience that we aren't aware or in control of is dreaming.

t_ch: 或许，黄金替罪羊就是某个已经陷入沉睡的泰坦的梦境……我们不能想当然地就否定这种猜想。说不定做梦就是泰坦的思考方式呢！
t_en: Maybe the Golden Scapegoat is the dream of a slumbering Titan... It's a conjecture that we can't just rule out. Perhaps dreaming is how Titans think!

t_ch: 啊——没关系，这个实验不需要你出手，你只要当个见证人，避免偶然路过的谁不小心把我叫醒，别让实验意外被打断就好。
t_en: And don't worry, I'm not going to ask you to participate in the actual experience. Just be my witness, and make sure no one accidentally interrupts the experiment by waking me up.

t_ch: （无奈地耸耸肩膀。）
t_en: *shrugs in resignation*

t_ch: （敷衍地耸耸肩膀。）
t_en: *shrugs nonchalantly*

t_ch: 恩维尔麻利地带上了特质的细铜丝帽子，躺下以后迅速地睡着了。你不禁疑惑，拥有如此强悍的入睡能力，这是否意味着他平日里对塞纳托斯的「称颂」真的起了效果？
t_en: Avilius puts on the special hat made from thin copper strands, and lay down before quickly falling asleep. Seeing how little effort it takes him to fall asleep, you can't help but wonder if this is the effect of his usual praises for Thanatos.

t_ch: 一种嫉妒的心情在你的心头蔓延开来。考虑到自己这个时不时废寝忘食的大忙人不可能真陪他在这里虚耗时间，你给那位「提灯找人」老师发个了「快来照顾这个傻子」的消息，然后就迅速离开了。
t_en: A hint of jealousy begins spreading within you. You're so busy that you sometimes don't even have time to eat or sleep, so why are you wasting your time here? You send a message to Scholar Light-Hunter to "come and look after this idiot" before swiftly making an exit.

t_ch: 数个小时后……
t_en: Several hours later...

t_ch: 哎呀，这不是{NICKNAME}嘛！这下可巧了，正好正好，我的老师、还有老师的老师，都和你介绍一下！
t_en: Ah, isn't this {NICKNAME}! What a coincidence! Perfect timing — let me introduce you to my teacher and my teacher's teacher!

t_ch: 这位安提基色拉人，就是我老师的老师，来古士老师。在整个奥赫玛，能流利诵读古泰坦语的学者，也只有他一人而已。
t_en: This is my teacher's teacher, the Antikytheran scholar, Mr. Lygus. He's the only one in all of Okhema who can fluently recite ancient Titan texts.

t_ch: 恩维尔同学谬赞了。只是安提基色拉人天然擅长记住各种零碎的知识，所以能帮助奥赫玛做一些力所能及的工作。
t_en: Avilius, you flatter me. Antikytherans are naturally good at remembering bits and pieces of knowledge, so I simply assist Okhema in ways I can.

t_ch: 很高兴认识你，来古士…
t_en: It's a pleasure to meet you, Lygus...

t_ch: 很高兴认识你{NICKNAME}，想必我们将来会有更多的缘分。
t_en: Pleased to meet you too, {NICKNAME}. I'm sure we'll cross paths more often in the future.

t_ch: 我认识来古士，可是…
t_en: I know Lygus, but...

t_ch: 没错，{NICKNAME}与我见过面的。切莫惊奇，我只是个古泰坦语爱好者罢了。
t_en: Indeed, {NICKNAME} and I have met before. Don't be surprised. I'm just an enthusiast of ancient Titan language, nothing more.

t_ch: 顺便，我就是在传信石版上那位「老师」，帮你解读「黄金替罪羊」所含信息的人。
t_en: By the way, I'm the teacher mentioned on the teleslate, the one who helped you decipher the information about the "Golden Scapegoat."

t_ch: 哦对对对，正式介绍一下，这位第欧根娜小姐就是传信石版上的「提灯找人」老师，成功翻译出<unbreak>20</unbreak>段《黄金替罪羊的呓语》的天才少女学者！
t_en: Oh right, let me formally introduce this young lady — Miss Diogena, the "Light-Hunter" mentioned on the teleslate. She's a brilliant scholar who translated 20 passages in Golden Scapegoat's Mutterings!

t_ch: …你说话怎么越来越离谱了。算起来，这好像还是我们第一次在正式场合见面啊，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: ...What you're saying is getting more and more exaggerated. Come to think of it, I believe this is the first time we've officially met, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 看来大家聊得很投机呢，不错不错。寒暄就聊到这里，让我们言归正传吧。
t_en: It seems everyone is enjoying the conversation. Good, good. Let's cut the small talk and get to the point.

t_ch: ——阁下，我能否借「头」一用？
t_en: — Excuse me, may I borrow a "head"?

t_ch: 只有「枕头」可以吗？
t_en: Will a pinhead do the work?

t_ch: 不行，我的头比较铁。
t_en: Not really. My head is just not in the game.

t_ch: …突然就玩黑暗兵法？
t_en: ...It got dark all of a sudden.

t_ch: 啊，您误会了…我是说「艾吉克拉尼翁」——您从「黄金替罪羊」那里收获的羊头。
t_en: Ah, it's nothing like that. I meant Aegikranion — the ram's head you recovered from the "Golden Scapegoat."

t_ch: 如您所见，这两面炫耀狩猎成果的标本墙稍显空旷。就在去年的例行粉刷中，终于被那位凯妮斯大人下令弃置了。
t_en: As you can see, the two trophy walls showcasing hunting achievements are looking a bit sparse. During last year's routine repainting, Lady Caenis ordered to have them removed.

t_ch: 而在下是个恋旧的人，颇不习惯这面墙就此空出…正好，第欧根娜和我说起了你们调查「黄金替罪羊」的活动——
t_en: But I'm quite sentimental and dislike leaving the walls empty... So, when Diogena mentioned your investigation of the "Golden Scapegoat" —

t_ch: 在下就想，你们的战利品好像正适合在这里展出？放心，作为回报，我也会协助你们，一同解读那些疑似来自遥远时代的神秘信息。
t_en: I thought your trophies might be perfect for display here. Don't worry, in return, I'll assist you in decoding the mysterious information that seems to come from a distant era.

t_ch: 真的吗？这可真是如虎添翼啊！{NICKNAME}，考虑一下？
t_en: Really? That would be a huge help! {NICKNAME}, what do you think?

t_ch: 倒也没什么问题。
t_en: I don't see any problem with that.

t_ch: 那真是太好了。不如先借我<unbreak>14</unbreak>个如何？每天两两一换，既可以轮流展示，也有足够的数量在手头研究。
t_en: That's fantastic! How about lending me <unbreak>14</unbreak> trophies? We can swap out two each day — it'll keep the exhibits fresh while ensuring I have enough for research.

t_ch: 那么，这些是我代表元老院向您支付的展品租借费用，请收好。
t_en: Here's the payment I'm making on behalf of Council of Elders for borrowing the exhibits. Please accept it.

t_ch: 不行，我不能亏。
t_en: No way, I'm not taking a loss here.

t_ch: 哈哈，既然是代表元老院，租借展品所需费用，我当然会如数奉上。
t_en: Haha, since it's the Council of Elders, I'll pay the full fee for borrowing the exhibits.

t_ch: 那么，就借我<unbreak>14</unbreak>个如何？每天两两一换，既可以轮流展示，也有足够的数量在手头研究。
t_en: In that case, how about lending me <unbreak>14</unbreak> trophies? We can swap out two each day — it'll keep the exhibits fresh while ensuring I have enough for research.

t_ch: 嗯，效果不错。那么，在下会尽快着手解读手里这些战利品了。
t_en: Alright, that works well. I'll get started on analyzing these trophies right away.

t_ch: 太谢谢您了，来古士老师！
t_en: Thank you so much, Mr. Lygus!

t_ch: 别急着谢我，哦…对了，虽然这话说出来多少显得对树庭缺乏尊重，不过——
t_en: Don't thank me just yet. Oh, by the way... though I mean no disrespect to the Grove... but—

t_ch: {NICKNAME}阁下，您可否帮我借树庭的「瑟希斯之心」一用？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, could you help me borrow the "Heart of Cerces" from the Grove?

t_ch: 可是…{F#她}{M#他}甚至不是树庭的学生…再说那个心，比人大多了……
t_en: But... {F#she}{M#he} isn't even a student of the Grove... Besides, that heart is much bigger than a person...

t_ch: 哈哈，我当然不是让{NICKNAME}阁下搬走它——
t_en: Haha, of course I'm not asking {NICKNAME} to move it physically—

t_ch: {NICKNAME}阁下，请收好这件小装置。如果您有空路过树庭，可以在「瑟希斯之心」晶体的下方找到一个小小的插口——刚好符合插入这件装置的规格。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, please take this little device. If you pass through the Grove, you'll find a small slot under the "Heart of Cerces" crystal. This device should fit perfectly.

t_ch: 您只要将此装置插入其中，略等片刻，让绿灯变成红灯，「瑟希斯之心」就可以对各位之前的翻译结果做一次校对，看看我们的研究出发点是否有所偏颇。
t_en: Just insert this device into the slot and wait a moment. Once the green light turns red, the "Heart of Cerces" will check our previous translations to see if there were any biases in our research.

t_ch: 如果一切顺利…今后我们就可以进一步利用它的算力来解析「黄金替罪羊」之谜。
t_en: If all goes well... we might even be able to leverage its computational power to further analyze the mystery of the "Golden Scapegoat."

t_ch: 哇！不过，树庭真的会同意让我们……？
t_en: Wow! But... will the Grove really let us...?

t_ch: 恩维尔。树庭…不久前遭遇了黑潮的袭击……
t_en: Avilius. The Grove... it was recently attacked by the black tide...

t_ch: 抱歉…愿{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}赐予他们平静的安眠。
t_en: My condolences... May {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} grant them eternal peace.

t_ch: 恩维尔少见地长叹一声。
t_en: Avilius sighed deeply, an unusual gesture for him.

t_ch: 利用「瑟希斯之心」核对我们研究成果的事就拜托你了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I'll leave the task of verifying our research with the "Heart of Cerces" in your hands, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 将机械插入「瑟希斯之心」后，它上面亮起了一盏类似发光二极管的小小绿灯。按来古士的说法，接下来只要「略等片刻」即可……
t_en: After inserting the device into the "Heart of Cerces," a small LED-like green light flickers on. According to Lygus, all that's left to do now is to "wait a little while..."

t_ch: …几分钟后。
t_en: ...A few minutes later.

t_ch: 绿灯的确变成了红灯。只是除此之外，你也感受不到任何其他的变化。看来，只有来古士本人才能对结果加以解析……
t_en: The green light indeed turned red. Other than that, you couldn't sense any changes. It seems only Lygus can interpret the results...

t_ch: 哦？看来「瑟希斯之心」已经帮我们计算出了结果？
t_en: Oh? It seems the "Heart of Cerces" has completed the calculations for us?

t_ch: 看起来是的。
t_en: Looks like it.

t_ch: 至少绿灯变红灯了。
t_en: At least the green light turned red.

t_ch: 感谢阁下。请稍等，我可以把它插入自己的身体，读取计算结果。
t_en: Thank you so much. Please wait a moment. I'll plug it into my system to read the results.

t_ch: 抱歉…事情好像并不顺利。
t_en: Apologies... it seems something has gone wrong.

t_ch: 发生什么了？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 万万没想到，竟然有人对「瑟希斯之心」动过手脚。这么说吧——「瑟希斯之心」为我们计算出的结果，是一片空白。
t_en: Unbelievable... Someone has tampered with the "Heart of Cerces." Let me put it this way — what it calculated for us was... nothing.

t_ch: …后续我们还能不能从{RUBY_B#羊头战利品}「艾吉克拉尼翁」{RUBY_E#}当中解读出新的信息，或许都很难说了。
t_en: ...At this rate, whether we can uncover any new information from {RUBY_B#ram-head treasure}Aegikranion{RUBY_E#} remains uncertain.

t_ch: 这也是黑潮的影响？
t_en: Is this also a result of the black tide?

t_ch: 什么人会对这个下手？
t_en: Who would do such a thing?

t_ch: 不知道。但按照有限的情报推断，最有可能的…是那个毁灭树庭的恐怖剑士。除了那缺乏理智的黑潮怪物，没人会故意去毁坏「瑟希斯之心」。
t_en: I'm not sure. But based on the limited information we have, the most likely culprit is the terrifying swordmaster who destroyed the Grove. Other than the mindless monsters of the black tide, no one else would deliberately damage the "Heart of Cerces."

t_ch: 没有理智的怪物…能做到这样的事情？
t_en: Mindless monsters... capable of doing something like this?

t_ch: 不好说。也许这同样是黑潮的某种机制…比如那剑士或怪物只是随便摸了摸「瑟希斯之心」，就可以造成这种破坏性的效果。
t_en: Hard to say. Maybe it's just another mechanism of the black tide. For instance, the swordmaster or the monsters might have only brushed against the "Heart of Cerces," yet caused this level of damage.

t_ch: 抱歉，在下不仅没有帮到你们，反而把事情搞砸了。
t_en: I'm sorry. Instead of helping, I seem to have made things worse.

t_ch: 我不会怪你。
t_en: I don't blame you.

t_ch: 感谢阁下的宽宏大量。无论如何，在下还是会为后续的标本支付租借费用。
t_en: Thank you for your understanding. Regardless, I will still cover the borrowing costs for the remaining specimens as compensation.

t_ch: 你白白让我跑腿！
t_en: You've had me running errands for nothing!

t_ch: 阁下批评得是。的确是在下弄巧成拙。无论如何，在下还是会为后续的标本支付租借费用，以此聊作补偿。
t_en: Your criticism is valid. I had truly messed this up. Even so, I'll ensure the borrowing fees for the remaining specimens are covered as a small gesture of apology.

t_ch: 感觉事情没这么简单……
t_en: This doesn't feel that simple...

t_ch: 就像第欧根娜所质疑的，在下也不认为那位恐怖剑士拥有此等算计。只不过……
t_en: Just like Diogena's doubts, I also don't think that terrifying swordmaster is capable of such calculated actions. But still...

t_ch: 从某种意义上说，黑潮和那位恐怖剑士，这些都是只是明面上的敌人。它们或许都只是现象。某种由世界真正的威胁所连带产生的现象。
t_en: In a way, the black tide and that swordmaster are just threats on the surface. They might be symptoms of a greater danger looming over the world.

t_ch: 在发现新的手段之前，我们的解密之旅恐怕要到此为止了。很遗憾没能帮助阁下解明「黄金替罪羊」背后的秘辛。
t_en: Until we discover new methods, it seems our journey of decryption has reached its end. I regret not being able to help you uncover the secrets behind the "Golden Scapegoat."

t_ch: 阁下可是仍有不甘？
t_en: Are you unwilling to let this go?

t_ch: 在下听说，{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}偶尔会说出善意的谎言——比如说，有时候人们的行动不符合预言，过早地逼近了某些真相。
t_en: I've heard stories about {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}. Sometimes they speak kind lies — like when people's actions veer too close to a truth that wasn't meant to be uncovered.

t_ch: 每当学生的研究陷入瓶颈的时候，我都会这么宽慰他们。
t_en: That's how I tell my students whenever they hit a roadblock in their research.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}阁下…我相信，这些话对你也拥有类似的价值。您不妨告诉自己——
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I believe these words hold value for you as well. Perhaps you should tell yourself—

t_ch: 是某位看不见的「世界的守护者」抹去了那些数据。仅此而已。
t_en: Some unseen "Guardian of the World" erased those results. And that's all there is to it.

t_ch: 唉，来古士爷爷总是这样，一旦对事情失去了兴趣，就不再关注它了。不过，如果你还有更多的战利品，还是可以交给我。
t_en: *sigh* Mr. Lygus is always like this. The moment he loses interest in something, he completely ignores it. Anyway, if you have more trophies, feel free to hand them over to me.

t_ch: 唔，储备的战利品超过了7个呢。很好，再帮你挂一个上去吧。
t_en: Oh, you've gathered more than seven trophies? Perfect, let me add another to the display.

t_ch: 第欧根娜找来帮手把新的战利品展示在标本墙上。
t_en: Diogena enlisted help to mount the new trophy on the specimen wall.

t_ch: 好的，我收到了。按来古士爷爷之前的规矩，七个凑一组才好轮展。这次还没凑够，我就先不帮你挂上去了哦？
t_en: Alright, got it. But according to Mr. Lygus's rules, trophies should be grouped in sets of seven for proper rotation. Since you don't have enough yet, I won't hang this one up for now.

t_ch: 说起来。之前来古士爷爷的说法，让我产生了一种感觉——
t_en: You know, Mr. Lygus's earlier remarks gave me a certain impression—

t_ch: 也许我们翻译出的《黄金替罪羊的呓语》，就和那个毁灭树庭的剑士有关？意味着那个人的某种过去？
t_en: Maybe our translations of Golden Scapegoat's Mutterings are somehow connected to the swordmaster who destroyed the Grove? Could they reflect something from that person's past?

t_ch: 也许有这种可能。
t_en: It's possible.

t_ch: 对吧？只可惜我们没有任何证据。
t_en: Right? Too bad we don't have any evidence.

t_ch: 难讲，毕竟没有任何证据。
t_en: Hard to say. Without evidence, it's just speculation.

t_ch: …唉，就像之前我告诫恩维尔的那样，光靠猜测还是解决不了问题啊。
t_en: *sigh* Just like I reminded Avilius earlier, speculation alone won't solve anything.

t_ch: 前面的那块大晶体…「瑟希斯之心」就在这里。
t_en: That big crystal up ahead... That's the "Heart of Cerces."

t_ch: 经历过许多轮回的冒险，你应该不会对一只会吐出机械音的羊头感到奇怪。
t_en: After countless cycles of adventure, surely you won't be surprised by a goat's head speaking in mechanical tones.

t_ch: 我是最初且最后的替罪羊。8是一个神圣的数字：你可以用8个战利品在我这里换到额外一步的机会。
t_en: I am the first and the final scapegoat. Eight is a sacred number: with 8 trophies, you may trade for one extra step here.

t_ch: 把那些和我长得一样的战利品都贡献给我。我将帮助你赢取最终的胜利。
t_en: Bring me the trophies that bear my likeness, and I shall aid you in claiming your ultimate victory.

t_ch: 《落木逐火英雄纪》
t_en: Heroic Saga of Flame-Chase

t_ch: 《门扉之启，王座之终》
t_en: Light Slips the Gate, Shadow Greets the Throne

t_ch: 《走过安眠地的花丛》
t_en: Through the Petals in the Land of Repose

t_ch: 《在黎明升起时坠落》
t_en: The Fall at Dawn's Rise

t_ch: 《因为太阳将要毁伤》
t_en: For the Sun is Set to Die

t_ch: 《英雄未死之前》
t_en: Before Their Deaths

t_ch: 《于长夜重返大地》
t_en: Back to Earth in Evernight

t_ch: 《成为昨日的明天》
t_en: As Tomorrow Became Yesterday

t_ch: 再见了，所有的替罪羊。
t_en: Farewell to all the scapegoats.

t_ch: 去获得来自天外的步数吧…它将助你赢取最终的胜利。
t_en: Go get those moves from beyond the sky... They will help you achieve ultimate victory.

t_ch: 「拾起一根羽毛，就能想象出鸟的声音；所以一根羽毛就是一只唱歌的鸟。」
t_en: "Pick up a feather, and you can imagine a bird's song. Therefore, a single feather is a singing bird."

t_ch: 构造出我的人说，他希望你喜欢这句话——虽然这不是他的创作。他还说，我应该问你，你是否想知道他的名字——因为你可能会不喜欢。
t_en: The one who built me said he hoped you'd enjoy this line, though it was not his own. He also said I should ask if you wish to know his name... for you may not like it.

t_ch: 你想知道他的名字吗？
t_en: Do you wish to know his name?

t_ch: 想
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 「吕枯耳戈斯」。如果你不喜欢这个名字，我对此表示抱歉。
t_en: "Lycurgus." If that name displeases you, I apologize.

t_ch: 不想
t_en: No.

t_ch: 我想，这确实是更正确的选择。
t_en: I think that is, indeed, the wiser choice.

t_ch: 「黄金替罪羊」的记录就到此为止了。再见。
t_en: Thus ends the record of the Golden Scapegoat. Farewell.

t_ch: 你从树洞中滑到了下层……
t_en: You slip through a hollow in the tree and descend to the lower level...

t_ch: 神奇的气流将你带到了上层……
t_en: A strange current of air lifts you up to the higher level...

t_ch: 错认了？的确，我从未在殿内见过你…圣殿把守严密，你是如何进到这里的？
t_en: Someone else? Indeed, I've never seen you in the sanctum before... The temple is well-guarded. How did you manage to get in?

t_ch: 好的，我这就去工作。
t_en: Alright, I'll just start working.

t_ch: …等等，我好像没在殿内见过你？圣殿把守严密，你是如何进到这里的？
t_en: Wait, I've never seen you in the sanctum before... The temple is well-guarded. How did you manage to get in?

t_ch: 也或许…是{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}给出的许可。请问，你对这墙上的装置可有了解？
t_en: Or perhaps... it's a sign of permission granted by {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#}. Tell me, do you know anything about the mechanism on this wall?

t_ch: 先前玩过两次。
t_en: Played that game twice before.

t_ch: 门径的禁制居然没拦住你，并且…将庞杂的溯寻推演称作玩乐，或许你就是我要找的人。
t_en: The containment mechanism didn't stop you, and you even call the intricate extrapolating process a game... Perhaps you're exactly the person I've been looking for.

t_ch: 记得是…门锁？
t_en: Isn't it... a door lock?

t_ch: 这话可不能乱说啊…不过，居然连内情都有所掌握，或许你就是我要找的人。
t_en: That's not something you should say lightly. Still, if you're privy to such details, perhaps you really are the one I need.

t_ch: 这器物名为「雅努斯迷津」，是{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}万径之门{RUBY_E#}的卜筮用具，用它可溯寻一切事物的所在地。本月的委托积压了许多，该来的辅祭却杳无音信。而你恰好在此时现身……
t_en: This device is called Janus' Maze, a divination tool of the {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Gate of Infinity{RUBY_E#}. It can trace the location of anything. There's a backlog of requests this month, and the acolyte meant to handle them is nowhere to be found. And now you appear...

t_ch: 懂了，我上。
t_en: Got it. I'm on it.

t_ch: 理解力不错。不觉得这就是命运泰坦们的安排吗？放心，报酬少不了你的。
t_en: That's some quick understanding. Don't you think this is the arrangement of the Fate Titans? Don't worry, you'll be well-compensated.

t_ch: 我还有别的事…
t_en: I have other things to do...

t_ch: 无妨，若是什么时候有兴趣了，回来找我就行。
t_en: That's fine. If you ever feel interested, come back and find me.

t_ch: 来，百闻不如一试，去迷津前就位吧。我用简单的问询作为样例，让你先熟悉下。
t_en: Now, let's give it a try. Stand in front of the Maze. I'll use a simple question as an example to get you familiar.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}万径之门{RUBY_E#}啊，请指引我们——「我昨日留存的小甜饼现在何处？」
t_en: O {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Gate of Infinity{RUBY_E#}, please guide us — "Where is the cookie I saved yesterday?"

t_ch: 迷津隆隆作响……
t_en: The Maze groans deeply...

t_ch: 迷津展露，交给你了。
t_en: The Maze has been revealed. I'll leave it to you.

t_ch: 来得太迟了，辅祭，迅速就位。还有数个失物委托，正等候被溯寻。
t_en: You're late, acolyte. Quickly, take your position. Several lost-item requests are still waiting to be traced.

t_ch: 认错人啦。
t_en: You must have mistaken me for someone else.

t_ch: 你果然有天赋。这便是迷津的推演流程，提出问题，推演路径，得到解答。而我要做的，是将路径化作文法，呈现在石板上，且看——
t_en: You've got talent. This is how the extrapolation works: Pose a question, deduce the path, and find the answer. My job is to transcribe the path into text on the slate. Let's see —

t_ch: …婆婆的甜饼可是连先代圣女都赞叹不已的美味，我本想留到日后慢慢享用的…过后我要去找那个祭司算账。
t_en: ...That grandma's cookies are a delicacy even the former Holy Maiden praised. I was saving them to enjoy later... Now I'll need to have a strong word with that priest.

t_ch: 趁热打铁，我们再做一次练习，这次就来测算——「真正的辅祭身在何处？」
t_en: Let's try another practice. This time, let's trace — "Where is the true acolyte?"

t_ch: {RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}万径之门{RUBY_E#}啊，请指引我们——
t_en: O {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Gate of Infinity{RUBY_E#}, please guide us —

t_ch: 迷津隆隆作响……
t_en: The Maze groans deeply...

t_ch: 迷津展露，交给你了。
t_en: The Maze has been revealed. I'll leave it to you.

t_ch: 不错，溯寻顺利完成了，我们来看看结果吧。
t_en: Excellent, the extrapolation is complete. Let's see the result.

t_ch: …{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}在上，我宣布，辅祭的职位出现了空缺，而你得到了它，恭喜你。
t_en: ...{RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} above! I declare the acolyte position vacant. Congratulations, you're now appointed.

t_ch: 你已经完全理解「雅努斯迷津」的机制了，现在，是时候处理一些真正的委托了，我瞧瞧……
t_en: You've mastered the mechanism of Janus' Maze. It's time to tackle real requests. Let me check...

t_ch: 来自信徒丝西涅——「我父亲的智力去哪里了？」
t_en: From a follower named Siphone — "Where has my father's intelligence gone?"

t_ch: 好抽象的问题。
t_en: That's quite abstract.

t_ch: 该不会是老年痴呆…
t_en: Could it be dementia...

t_ch: …很遗憾，我认识这位信徒，她的父亲确实年迈而思维阻滞，但若是{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}万径之门{RUBY_E#}，将其寻回或也并非妄言。就位吧，辅祭。
t_en: ...Unfortunately, I know this follower. Her father is indeed elderly and mentally diminished. But if the {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Gate of Infinity{RUBY_E#} wills it, recovering the father's intellect may not be impossible. Take your position, acolyte.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}万径之门{RUBY_E#}啊，请指引我们——
t_en: O {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Gate of Infinity{RUBY_E#}, please guide us —

t_ch: 迷津隆隆作响……
t_en: The Maze groans deeply...

t_ch: 迷津展露，交给你了。
t_en: The Maze has been revealed. I'll leave it to you.

t_ch: 做得好，辅祭，这是你值得纪念的第一个委托，来看看拓写出的结果吧。
t_en: Well done, acolyte. This is your first request, a notable occasion. Let's see the transcription.

t_ch: 飞走了…
t_en: It flew away...

t_ch: 有够敷衍的。
t_en: Quite unhelpful.

t_ch: …这肯定不是委托人想要的答案。幸好，先前出现类似状况时，我研究过补救方法。只需细化问题后再次提问即可。由我来……
t_en: This surely isn't the answer the client wants. Luckily, I've studied remedial methods for such cases. By refining the question and asking again, we may get a better result. Allow me —

t_ch: {RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}万径之门{RUBY_E#}，请指引我们——「信徒丝西涅的父亲，其脑中的学识飞去了何处？」
t_en: O {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Gate of Infinity{RUBY_E#}, please guide us — "Where has the knowledge in Siphone's father's mind flown to?"

t_ch: 迷津隆隆作响……
t_en: The Maze groans deeply...

t_ch: 好了，再试一次吧，辅祭。随着问题的深入，溯寻会变得更困难，但我相信，你能顺利解决的。
t_en: All right, try again, acolyte. As the question deepens, the extrapolation becomes more complex, but I believe you can manage.

t_ch: 你已经融会贯通了，辅祭。我在拓写时便能感知到，这次的预兆更加清晰，让我们看看。
t_en: You've fully grasped it, acolyte. I can sense it in the transcription — this omen is clearer. Let's see.

t_ch: 也没好到哪去啊！
t_en: Still not much better!

t_ch: 世界各地…
t_en: Scattered across the world...

t_ch: 看来，即使动用{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}的权能，对于不复存在之物，也只能言尽于此了…但我有种预感，这个结果或许不算太差。
t_en: It seems that even with {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#}'s power, what no longer exists cannot be entirely restored. Yet, I have a feeling this outcome may not be as bad as it seems.

t_ch: 辅祭，把这石板拿去交给委托人吧，她就在楼下。
t_en: Acolyte, take this slate to the client. She's just downstairs.

t_ch: 辅祭，把这石板拿去交给委托人吧，她就在楼下。
t_en: Acolyte, take this slate to the client. She's just downstairs.

t_ch: 你好，有什么事吗？
t_en: Hello, can I help you?

t_ch: 我来交付雅努斯的神谕。
t_en: I've come to deliver Janus's prophecy.

t_ch: 啊，原来是祭司大人！家父已经失智许久了，请告诉我，在哪里能寻回他的智慧？
t_en: Ah, you're the priest! My father has been senile for a long time now. Please, tell me, where can I find his wisdom?

t_ch: 原来…如此。{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}在上，请接受我的谢意。
t_en: So... this is the answer. {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#}, I offer you my gratitude.

t_ch: 搞明白什么了吗？
t_en: Did you figure something out?

t_ch: 我没懂…
t_en: I don't quite get it...

t_ch: 祭司大人，您有所不知。家父在因身体欠佳而长休前，曾是一位著名的讲师，教导过的门生不计其数。
t_en: Priest, you might not know this, but my father was a renowned lecturer who taught countless students before retiring due to poor health.

t_ch: 而{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}的回应，毫无疑问是在点醒愚笨的我，父亲的学识早已在世间各处开花结果…他是位伟大的人。
t_en: And {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#}'s response is undoubtedly reminding me that my father's wisdom has long taken root all over the world through his students... He was a great man.

t_ch: 无论如何，赞美{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}，也感谢您的付出。我打算联系他的学生，或许见见老面孔，能让他的症状得到改善。请收下这些吧，谨以表达我的谢意。
t_en: In any case, praise {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#}, and thank you for your efforts. I plan to contact his students — perhaps meeting some old faces might help improve his condition. Please accept this as a token of my gratitude.

t_ch: 对报酬还满意吗？我们继续吧。
t_en: Are you satisfied with the reward? Let's continue.

t_ch: 接下来的委托…来自一位守城的卫士，内容是——「我的真爱该在何处」。
t_en: The next request comes from a city guard. The content is — "Where is my true love?"

t_ch: 这也能找？
t_en: Janus can find that?

t_ch: …实话说，我也没有太大把握。情爱相关理应依靠墨涅塔的权能…总之，相信伟大的{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}，先试试看吧。
t_en: To be honest, I'm not entirely confident about it either. Matters of love should ideally fall under the domain of Mnestia. Still, let's trust the great {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} and give it a try.

t_ch: 他的恋人失踪了？
t_en: Did his lover go missing?

t_ch: 恐怕不是这个意思…就我所知，这位卫兵始终是单身。这是求缘求错泰坦，求到{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}万径之门{RUBY_E#}来了…也罢，相信伟大的{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}，先试试看吧。
t_en: I don't think so... As far as I know, this guard has always been single. It seems they sought love from the wrong Titan and ended up at {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#}'s temple. No matter, let's trust in the great {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} and proceed.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}万径之门{RUBY_E#}，请指引我们——
t_en: O {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Gate of Infinity{RUBY_E#}, please guide us —

t_ch: 迷津隆隆作响……
t_en: The Maze groans deeply...

t_ch: 迷津展露了。完成你的工作吧，辅祭。
t_en: The Maze has been revealed. Do your job, acolyte.

t_ch: 你越来越娴熟了，辅祭，有兴趣在圣殿正式就职吗？先来看看石板上的结果吧。
t_en: You're getting better at this, acolyte. Have you considered formally joining the temple? First, let's look at the results on the slate.

t_ch: 不出意外，需要继续追问才能得到解答。伟大的{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}，请赐予信徒更详尽的指示——
t_en: As expected, we'll need to refine the question for a clearer answer. Great {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#}, please grant our follower more detailed guidance —

t_ch: 迷津隆隆作响……
t_en: The Maze groans deeply...

t_ch: 交给你了，辅祭。
t_en: I'll leave it to you, acolyte.

t_ch: 我们来看看……
t_en: Let's see.

t_ch: 如此精确的指示，想必那位信徒应该能满意…石板托付于你了，去交给他吧。
t_en: Such precise directions — surely this will satisfy the follower. The slate is yours to deliver.

t_ch: 辅祭，石板托付于你了，去交给那位卫兵吧。
t_en: Acolyte, please deliver the slate to that guard.

t_ch: 阁下，你这是……
t_en: Um, what brings you here?

t_ch: 我来交付雅努斯的神谕。
t_en: I've come to deliver Janus's prophecy.

t_ch: 噢噢！感谢阁下！第十次的委托，终于有所回应了！请让我拜领神谕！
t_en: Oh! Thank you, great one! This is the tenth time I've submitted a request, and finally, there's an answer! Please, let me receive the prophecy!

t_ch: <unbreak>177</unbreak>号房…不不不，那是我老朋友的住处，我连她的脸都看腻了。我们之间绝对没可能。
t_en: Room <unbreak>177</unbreak>... No, no, that's my old friend's place. I've seen her face too often. There's no way it's her!

t_ch: 可泰坦的神谕绝不会出错，可她，我、我应该…如何是好啊！
t_en: But the Titan's prophecy cannot be wrong. Yet... she... What... What should I do!?

t_ch: 这位虔诚的卫兵估计要烦恼好一阵了，据说妨碍恋爱的人会被大地兽踢，还是悄悄离开吧。
t_en: This devout guard is likely to stew over this for a while. They say interfering in love can get you kicked by a dromas, so let's quietly slip away.

t_ch: 接下来是积压委托中的最后一件，也是唯一一件因报酬不足，且推演过于繁琐而被其他祭司放弃的委托。
t_en: Now for the final request in the backlog. This is the only request that the other priests abandoned due to insufficient pay and excessive complexity.
